guidance: The deliverable is curated news items (and the narrative chapters built from them); the sources are the archived full article text in the collection's sources/. Exactness is load-bearing: every quote in an item must be VERBATIM in a source, and every factual assertion (numbers, dates, who-said-what, capability claims) must be supported. Flag headline-style overstatement, quotes that drift from the source, and capability claims that garble the original announcement. This matches the repo's stated principle: Claude does the editorial verification, scripts only convert format.
note: Run per collection. Resolve {name} to e.g. 2026-05. Chapters reference items, so check items first; a chapter claim that traces to a verified item is fine.
